name: power_operator_signed_exponent_with_zero_base_x_propagation
description: Code should include a power operator (**) where the base is a single-bit signal that can be zero and the exponent is a signed signal that can be negative, triggering IEEE 1364-2005 x-propagation rules when base is zero and exponent is negative.
snippet:
wire signed [1:0] wire_4;
assign out = (1'b1 ** (zero_base ** wire_4));

name: power_operator_nested_with_signed_inner_exponent
description: Code should include nested power operators where the inner exponent is signed and can be negative, causing the inner power to evaluate to 'x when the base is zero, which should propagate to the outer result per IEEE 1364-2005.
